channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Standard Chartered to sell Nairobi East Africa headquarters as part of global restructuring
item.description: Standard Chartered Bank imeamua kuuza ofisi yake kuu ya East Africa iliyoko Nairobi, hatua hii ni sehemu ya mpango mkubwa wa benki hiyo kupunguza gharama na kuongeza ufanisi. Ofisi ya Chiromo Road, ye...

item.title: Tim Tszyu relocates to Miami training hub with Mike Tyson influence amid comeback plans
item.description: Australian boxer Tim Tszyu has relocated his training base to Miami, with boxing legend Mike Tyson playing a key role in fuelling the move. The former world champion is eyeing a strong return to the r...

item.title: Tomahawk Land Attack Missile Launched from Tacloban Hits Target in Nueva Ecija During Balikatan 2026 Exercises
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) successfully conducted a live-fire exercise with a United States Tomahawk Land Attack Missile (TLAM) as part of the 2026 Balikatan joint military drills. The ...

item.title: 13-year-old apprehended following violent incident targeting police in Londonderry
item.description: A 13-year-old boy has been taken into custody following a series of violent incidents in Londonderry, where petrol bombs, bottles, and fireworks were reportedly thrown at police officers. The attacks ...

item.title: India and Vietnam set trade plan with $25 billion target for 2030
item.description: India and Vietnam have formally agreed on a comprehensive trade roadmap to enhance bilateral economic cooperation, with a target of reaching USD 25 billion in trade by 2030. The two countries are focu...

item.title: Infantino justifies high 2026 World Cup ticket prices amid criticism from fans
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino has defended the pricing of tickets for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, following strong backlash from fan groups who argue that costs are excessively high. Organisations such...

item.title: DJ Maphorisa addresses fans after Scorpion Kings Live tickets sell out at FNB Stadium
item.description: Tickets for the upcoming Scorpion Kings Live concert at FNB Stadium on 19 September 2026 sold out almost immediately after going live on 5 May 2026, leaving thousands of fans disappointed. DJ Maphoris...

item.title: Mombasa ANU Arrests Two Suspects, Seizes 250kg of Bhang in Changamwe
item.description: In Mombasa, the Anti-Narcotics Unit (ANU) of the Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has stepped up efforts to fight drug trafficking along the coast. On Wednesday, 6th May 2026, the unit arr...

item.title: California Supreme Court Rules Against Unaffordable Bail as Pretrial Detention
item.description: The California Supreme Court has issued a unanimous ruling reinforcing that pretrial liberty should be the default, and pretrial detention should only occur under narrowly defined circumstances. The c...

item.title: Small Modular Reactors Face Challenges in Scaling and Standardization
item.description: Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) are often promoted as a quick solution for expanding nuclear power, but the evidence suggests they are not ready for broad commercial deployment. While nuclear power alre...

item.title: Election Commission prevents 68 lakh cyberattacks, ensures secure counting with ECINET
item.description: On the counting day of the recently concluded assembly elections across five states and union territories, the Election Commission (EC) successfully defended its online platforms against over 68 lakh ...

item.title: Ombudsman Office Receives Over 3,500 Applicants for 114 Vacancies Nationwide
item.description: At least 3,500 individuals have applied for 114 permanent positions at the Office of the Ombudsman, according to Assistant Ombudsman for Internal Affairs Luigi Bonoan. The vacancies are available at t...

item.title: Iranian FM Araqchi holds talks with China's Wang Yi ahead of Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi met with China's top diplomat Wang Yi in Beijing on Wednesday. This meeting highlights the strong relationship between Iran and China just days before US Preside...

item.title: Ontario special education teacher sentenced to three years for sexually exploiting a student
item.description: An Ontario high school special education teacher, Tony Paniccia, has been sentenced to three years in prison for sexually exploiting a student who was 31 years younger than him. Paniccia, 49, pleaded ...

item.title: Geoffrey Mosiria Intervenes to Rescue Abused Children in Tassia, Mother Taken into Custody
item.description: In a serious incident in Tassia, Embakasi East, Nairobi, Geoffrey Mosiria, the Nairobi County Chief Officer for Citizen Engagement and Customer Service, stepped in to rescue two children from alleged ...

item.title: Emily Roux discusses financial challenges and difficult restaurant interactions
item.description: The article centres on Emily Roux, a restaurateur, who reflects on the challenges of running a business that has not turned a profit. She shares her experiences of navigating difficult conversations w...

item.title: Understanding Voter Behaviour in Tamil Nadu: The Vijay Effect on Welfare Politics
item.description: The Tamil Nadu Assembly elections have brought forth an intriguing political phenomenon, popularly referred to as the 'Vijay Paradox'. Despite decades of welfare-oriented governance and extensive soci...

item.title: President Ruto Calls for Stronger Intra-Regional Trade to Drive EAC Economic Growth
item.description: President William Ruto has emphasised the big potential East African countries have in increasing trade among themselves to grow their economies. While speaking in the Tanzanian Parliament in Dodoma o...

item.title: Engineering Skills Shortage Threatens Africa’s Renewable Energy Expansion
item.description: Africa is poised for a renewable energy boom, yet the continent faces a major hurdle: a shortage of skilled materials engineers. Despite having abundant solar and renewable resources, many African pro...

item.title: Senate partial report exposes flood control corruption, recommends reforms
item.description: The Senate blue ribbon committee has released a partial report on multi-billion-peso flood control corruption, highlighting systemic weaknesses and misuse of government funds. Senator Panfilo Lacson p...
